notes_md:
**“Trân”** by itself isn’t really used alone in modern Vietnamese. You almost always see it in **“trân trọng”**, which means *to cherish, deeply value, appreciate*.

It’s a verb: use it when you truly value a person, thing, chance, or feeling. It’s stronger and more emotional than just *thích* (like).  

Strong synonym: **quý / quý trọng**. Clear opposite: **coi thường** (look down on, not value).  
You’ll also see **“Trân trọng,”** at the end of formal emails or letters, meaning *Respectfully*.

example.vi: Tôi rất trân trọng tình bạn của chúng ta.

definitions.en: To cherish or value something highly.
definitions.es: Apreciar o valorar algo en gran medida.
definitions.vi: Trân trọng hoặc đánh giá cao điều gì đó.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He always cherish beautiful memories with his family.
She knows how to cherish opportunities in life.
I want to learn how to cherish the time with friends more.
notes:
“Trân” by itself isn’t really used alone in modern Vietnamese. You almost always see it in “trân trọng”, which means to cherish, deeply value, appreciate.

It’s a verb: use it when you truly value a person, thing, chance, or feeling. It’s stronger and more emotional than just thích (like).  

Strong synonym: quý / quý trọng. Clear opposite: coi thường (look down on, not value).  
You’ll also see “Trân trọng,” at the end of formal emails or letters, meaning Respectfully.
exampleSentences: Anh luôn trân những kỷ niệm đẹp với gia đình.
Chị ấy biết cách trân những cơ hội trong cuộc sống.
Em muốn học cách trân thời gian bên bạn bè hơn.
